VAL-DS-005.evidence: File components/ui/AnimatedModal.tsx exists. Code inspection confirms: (a) isOpen/onClose props in AnimatedModalProps interface, (b) uses m.div from motion/react with AnimatePresence, (c) panel has initial={{ y: '100%' }} and animate={{ y: 0 }} for slide-up, (d) backdrop m.div with initial/animate/exit opacity transitions, (e) backdrop onClick={onClose} for close on backdrop click. Spring animation with damping=25, stiffness=300. Component is a complete reusable animated modal.
VAL-DS-005.details: The AnimatedModal component file exists at components/ui/AnimatedModal.tsx with all required features: isOpen/onClose props, Framer Motion (motion/react m.div) animations, slide-up panel via translateY (y: '100%' -> y: 0), backdrop fade in/out (opacity 0->1->0), and close on backdrop click. Note: The component is not yet imported in any page files (only in test files), but the assertion asks for the component to 'exist' with the specified features, which it does. The existing page modals (product detail, event detail) use inline div-based dialogs rather than this shared component.

AnimatedModalProps has isOpen: boolean, onClose: () => void, children: React.ReactNode
Imports { m, AnimatePresence } from 'motion/react'

Backdrop: initial={{ opacity: 0 }}, animate={{ opacity: 1 }}, exit={{ opacity: 0 }}
onClick={onClose} on backdrop m.div

VAL-DS-006.evidence: File components/ui/Toast.tsx exists. Code inspection confirms: (a) ToastType = 'success' | 'error' | 'info' with corresponding icons (CheckCircle, XCircle, Info from lucide-react), (b) auto-dismiss via setTimeout at 3000ms (3 seconds), (c) animate in/out via m.div with AnimatePresence (initial={{ y: -100, opacity: 0 }}, animate={{ y: 0, opacity: 1 }}, exit={{ opacity: 0 }}), (d) ToastProvider mounted globally in components/LayoutWrapper.tsx wrapping all children. useToast hook exported.
VAL-DS-006.details: Toast system is fully implemented with success/error/info types, 3-second auto-dismiss, animated entrance/exit, and globally mounted provider. The useToast hook provides a showToast(message, type) API. ToastProvider is wrapped in LayoutWrapper.tsx which wraps the entire app tree. Note: useToast is not currently called in any app page files (only in tests), but the provider IS mounted globally and the hook API IS available.

All data-fetching pages use appropriate skeleton variants during loading. Zero instances of animate-spin remain.

VAL-DS-008.evidence: File components/ui/Button.tsx exists with m.button from motion/react, whileTap={{ scale: 0.97 }}, variant prop supporting primary/secondary/outline/ghost, TypeScript ButtonProps interface. However, the Button component is NOT imported or used in any page file under app/ or components/ (only in __tests__/components/ui/Button.test.tsx). All pages still use inline <button> elements.
VAL-DS-008.details: The Button component itself is well-implemented with all required features: (a) Framer Motion whileTap={{ scale: isDisabled ? 1 : 0.97 }} on m.button, (b) variant prop with 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'outline' | 'ghost' variants each with distinct styling, (c) proper TypeScript interface (ButtonProps extends React.ButtonHTMLAttributes<HTMLButtonElement>). HOWEVER, the assertion requires 'used in at least one page replacing inline buttons' — and grep confirms Button is NOT imported in any app page. The login page (app/login/page.tsx line 170) uses an inline <button> with gradient styling. Product detail page (app/products/[id]/page.tsx) uses inline <button> elements for 'For sale' and 'Purchase'. Event detail page similarly uses inline <button> elements. The shared Button component exists but is unused outside its test file.
VAL-DS-008.issues: Button component is not imported or used in any page file. Assertion criterion (c) 'used in at least one page replacing inline buttons' is NOT met. Only __tests__/components/ui/Button.test.tsx imports it.
